era: Seattle, 2000s-present. Producer, multi-instrumentalist, recording engineer, visual artist. Solo work is atmospheric electronic/experimental. Produced for Shabazz Palaces, THEESatisfaction, and other Seattle artists.

location.origin: Seattle, Washington. Producer, multi-instrumentalist, recording engineer.
location.formative: Seattle's experimental and hip-hop communities. Studio as instrument. Production credits for Shabazz Palaces (Sub Pop), THEESatisfaction.
location.career: Erik Blood -- producer, multi-instrumentalist, recording engineer, visual artist. Solo work: atmospheric electronic/experimental. Produced for Shabazz Palaces, THEESatisfaction. Electronic/Experimental at Genre Stage 76. E=4. Producer-artist like Chris Walla (67).

labels: Self-released (solo work via Bandcamp and streaming; production credits on Sub Pop and other labels)

members.name: Erik Blood
members.note: Solo artist. Also recording engineer and producer for Shabazz Palaces, THEESatisfaction, and other Seattle artists. Visual artist. The studio is the instrument.

associated_musicians.name: Chris Walla
associated_musicians.note: Both recording engineers who became artists. Walla defined Death Cab's sound; Blood shaped Seattle's experimental and hip-hop scenes. Different genres, same methodology.

associated_musicians.name: Chong the Nomad
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic, both Seattle, both experimental. Chong is beat-driven production; Blood is atmospheric and genre-blending. Both operate in Seattle's electronic community.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Both experimental, both Seattle. Hovercraft is maximalist drone; Blood is atmospheric electronic with more structural variety. Both push boundaries.

associated_musicians.name: Sky Cries Mary
associated_musicians.note: Both atmospheric, both electronic. Sky Cries Mary is ambient/psychedelic; Blood is electronic/experimental with hip-hop influence.

associated_musicians.name: The Postal Service
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic. Postal Service is structured pop; Blood is experimental. Different relationships to structure and genre convention.

associated_musicians.name: Shabazz Palaces
associated_musicians.note: Ishmael Butler's experimental hip-hop project on Sub Pop. Blood produced albums. The Shabazz Palaces connection places Blood at the intersection of electronic and hip-hop communities.

associated_musicians.name: THEESatisfaction
associated_musicians.note: Seattle duo on Sub Pop. Blood produced. Another node in the cross-genre bridge.

theory_nodes.concept: The producer's palette (ninety-fifth acoustic element -- the sound produced when a recording engineer and producer makes music for themselves; the full vocabulary of studio techniques deployed not in service of another artist's vision but as the producer's own creative expression; the studio as instrument; no genre obligation; compression, effects chains, spatial processing, synthesis, sampling, layering)
examples: Erik Blood (degree 94) -- producer making solo music. The full studio vocabulary deployed for personal creative expression. The producer's palette.
Contrast with the shimmer veil (degree 93, NAVVI) -- reverb-as-architecture within dream pop. The producer's palette has no framework obligation.
Contrast with the producer's touch (degree 67, Chris Walla) -- shaping indie rock. The producer's palette is the producer untethered from genre.
The engine adds the sound of the producer working for themselves. Studio as open field.
theory_nodes.description: The producer's palette is the 95th acoustic element. Studio-as-instrument. Full production vocabulary. No genre obligation. The producer working for themselves.

ancestors: The shimmer veil (degree 93, NAVVI) -- reverb-as-architecture within dream pop. The producer's palette is genre-unbounded.
The producer's touch (degree 67, Chris Walla) -- shaping one genre. The producer's palette is the producer untethered.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-five acoustic elements across ninety-four degrees. Track element 96 at degree 95.

theory_nodes.concept: The cross-genre bridge (ninety-fifth artist-city pattern -- an artist who connects different genre communities through production and collaboration; not blending genres in their own music but literally working in multiple genre worlds; a node in the social graph linking scenes)
examples: Erik Blood (degree 94) -- bridges electronic, experimental, and hip-hop communities through production credits. The cross-genre bridge.
Shabazz Palaces (Sub Pop, experimental hip-hop) + solo electronic work = cross-genre node.
Contrast with the intimate machine (degree 93, NAVVI) -- about scale. The cross-genre bridge is about connection between communities.
theory_nodes.description: The cross-genre bridge is the 95th artist-city pattern. Cross-community connection through production. A node linking scenes.

ancestors: The intimate machine (degree 93, NAVVI) -- electronic intimacy. The cross-genre bridge is electronic connectivity.
The producer-as-artist (degree 67, Chris Walla) -- production within one genre. The cross-genre bridge is production across genres.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-five artist-city patterns across ninety-four degrees. Track pattern 96 at degree 95.

theory_nodes.concept: Electronic/Experimental at Genre Stage 76 -- studio-as-instrument, genre-blending, atmospheric; production techniques as compositional method; drawing from hip-hop, electronic, ambient, and art-rock
examples: Erik Blood (degree 94) -- Electronic/Experimental at Genre Stage 76.
Distinguished from Dream Pop/Electronic (NAVVI, 93) -- Blood is less pop, more experimental.
Distinguished from Ethereal/Electronic (Sky Cries Mary, 90) -- Blood has more structural variety and hip-hop influence.
Fifth electronic-adjacent genre in nine degrees (86, 90, 91, 93, 94).
theory_nodes.description: Electronic/Experimental at Genre Stage 76. Studio-as-instrument. Genre-blending. Atmospheric. Five electronic entries in nine degrees.

ancestors: Dream Pop/Electronic (degree 93) at Genre Stage 75. Genre stages continue.
Electronic Pop (degree 86, Postal Service). Different electronic approach.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 76 at degree 94. Track at 95.

theory_nodes.concept: E=4 at degree 94 -- producer visibility through credits; Shabazz Palaces (Sub Pop), THEESatisfaction; the production credits create visibility infrastructure that elevates the solo work
examples: Erik Blood (degree 94) -- E=4. Production credits for Sub Pop artists create visibility.
Higher energy than NAVVI (93, E=3) because of the production credit infrastructure.
The Shabazz Palaces connection places Blood in a visible position within Seattle's music community.
theory_nodes.description: E=4 at degree 94. Producer visibility through production credits. Shabazz Palaces, THEESatisfaction. Sub Pop connection.

ancestors: E=3 at degree 93 (NAVVI). Energy rises from 3 to 4.

theory_nodes.concept: Producer-as-artist lineage: Chris Walla (degree 67) and Erik Blood (degree 94) -- recording engineers who became solo artists; different genres, same methodology; the engineer as artist
examples: Chris Walla (degree 67) -- defined Death Cab's sound through production. Indie rock.
Erik Blood (degree 94) -- shaped Seattle's experimental and hip-hop scenes. Electronic/experimental.
Both recording engineers who became artists. Both approach music through the studio. Different genres, same methodology.
theory_nodes.description: Producer-as-artist lineage. Walla (67, indie rock) and Blood (94, experimental/hip-hop). Recording engineers who became artists.

ancestors: Chris Walla (degree 67) -- first producer-as-artist in the engine.
genealogy.relationship: Two producer-as-artists in 94 degrees. Track whether more appear.

cross_references.relationship: Producer-as-artist lineage. Both recording engineers who became artists. Walla shaped indie rock; Blood shaped experimental/hip-hop. Different genres, same methodology -- the engineer as artist.
Electronic Seattle parallel. Both electronic, both experimental. Chong is beat-driven; Blood is atmospheric and genre-blending. Both Seattle's electronic community.
Experimental connection. Both experimental, both Seattle. Hovercraft is maximalist drone; Blood is atmospheric electronic with more variety. Both boundary-pushers.
Atmospheric parallel. Both atmospheric, both electronic. Sky Cries Mary is ambient/psychedelic; Blood is electronic/experimental with hip-hop influence.
Electronic contrast. Postal Service is structured electronic pop; Blood is experimental electronic. Both electronic; different relationships to structure.
